codeReview.summary: The feature largely satisfies its intended behavior (Fly artifacts, preflight remediation paths, and deploy redaction checks), but it introduces a blocking shell-command injection risk by interpolating env-derived values directly into execSync command strings.

issues.description: `mors deploy` builds a shell command string using `${deployConfig.flyctlPath}`, `${deployConfig.appName}`, and `${deployConfig.primaryRegion}` and executes it via `execSync`. These values are env-derived (including `FLY_APP_NAME` and `MORS_DEPLOY_FLYCTL_PATH`) and are not quoted/escaped, enabling shell injection and arbitrary local command execution when crafted values are present.

Preflight auth check executes `execSync(`${flyctlPath} auth whoami`)` with an unvalidated `flyctlPath` that may come from `MORS_DEPLOY_FLYCTL_PATH`. Because this is passed as a shell string, metacharacters in the path can execute unintended commands during preflight.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`, but the skill requires manual CLI/API sanity checks for user-facing surfaces (or explicit rationale when skipped). This deploy feature added a user-facing CLI command, yet the handoff contains only automated commands and no manual-check rationale.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill procedure step 8 in `.factory/skills/mors-global-worker/SKILL.md` requires manual checks for user-facing changes. Handoff `2026-02-28T17-09-22-775Z__fly-deploy-artifacts-and-safe-placeholder-failures__0ed0b0ac-6086-4a51-80ca-139456945c7a.json` lists only `commandsRun` and sets `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared guidance documents do not explicitly codify safe subprocess invocation for env-derived values (e.g., avoid shell string interpolation and prefer argument-vector APIs).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS security constraints emphasize secret leakage prevention, but this feature introduced shell-string `execSync` command assembly at `src/cli.ts:2126` and `src/deploy.ts:190` using env-derived inputs.

summary: Review failed due to blocking shell-injection vulnerabilities in deploy command execution and preflight auth checks. Functional coverage is otherwise strong for artifact presence, remediation messaging, and secret-redaction-focused tests.
